structure.text:
Subject: RE: NCM-P-2025-09-29 HOPER Update
From: Nova Capital Markets <capitalmarkets@novahomeloans.com>
Date: 2025-09-29 11:42:19-07:00

 

ANNOUNCEMENT CORRECTION
 
In our recent (below) announcement regarding HOPER funds and their treatment under FHA guidelines, we incorrectly referred to HOPER funds as “incident to the transaction.” The correct designation is “inducement to the transaction.”  Thank you for your attention to this clarification.
 
ANNOUNCEMENT SUMMARY
 
Recently, HOPER sought legal counsel regarding the use of HOPER funds by the borrower for their minimum required investment (MRI). Counsel clarified that since enrollment in the private savings club offered by HOPER occurs alongside the purchase and installation of a solar system through HOPER, as well as the purchase of a new home using FHA financing, the HOPER funds can be considered “incident inducement to the transaction.” This designation makes HOPER an interested party to the transaction.
 
In light of this, HOPER has advised lenders to treat it as an interested party, which restricts the use of HOPER funds to no more than six percent of the sales price for the borrower’s closing costs and prepaids, in accordance with HUD guidelines for Interested Party Contributions. NOVA values the guidance provided by our partners at HOPER and has decided to adopt this approach moving forward. Any HOPER loan in process before October 1, 2025, may continue to utilize the earned income for down payment.  However, effective with applications dated on or after October 1, 2025, HOPER funds will be limited to the following uses, up to 6% of the purchase price:
 
1.            Closing costs
2.            Prepaids
3.            Permanent interest rate buydowns
 
We realize that, in addition to offering our borrowers access to clean energy, many of them also require downpayment assistance.  With that in mind, we’re excited to introduce our latest product called NOVA Smart Solar.  We are partnering with a company named Arcasa but will white label the program.
 
NOVA Smart Solar offers borrowers the same benefits as HOPER, with the added advantage of down payment assistance in the form of a grant or a forgivable second lien.  The standard maximum DPA is 5.5% of the purchase price or appraised value, whichever is lower.  In the scenarios where a second lien is used instead of a grant, the second liens have a 10-year term, no interest, no monthly payments and are forgiven after the solar is installed.  
 
As with HOPER, all FHA guidelines apply. Minimum FICO score is 580. The maximum loan amount is the purchase price plus solar cost, not to exceed 120% CLTV, and is subject to HUD’s county loan limits. Condos are not eligible. The program is not available in Alaska or Hawaii. Homebuyer education is not required.
 
We are excited to launch this program and look forward to the new partnership. NOVA will host a webinar to introduce the production team to NOVA Smart Solar, followed by in-branch training beginning mid-October. Product will share additional details about the program in a separate announcement, and Training will send webinar invitations soon.
** For NOVA Home Loans Internal Distribution Only **
For inquiries, contact:
Scenario Desk <mailto:Scenariodesk@novahomeloans.com>         Lock Desk <mailto:lock.desk@novahomeloans.com>         Product Department <mailto:Productdept@novahomeloans.com>
